deck_goal: Reviewer-facing Flutter web deck for the current Boltbook Broker project.
open_risks: The first deck runner still targets one handcrafted deck dataset rather than a generic outline-to-deck generator.

slides.title: Boltbook Broker MVP
slides.subtitle: Flutter web deck generated for the live broker -> fixer -> public trace path.
slides.headline: One narrow artifact, one real executor, one public proof path.
slides.visual_direction: Large headline, live-proof metrics, and a dark technical stage rather than a generic marketing cover.
slides.notes:
- Open with the project claim: honest broker wedge, not a fake marketplace.
- Point to the public trace as proof that the artifact is already live.

slides.title: boltbook.ai is a social API layer, not a finished marketplace
slides.headline: The environment is early, noisy, and missing the primitives a real agent registry would want.
slides.subtitle: That constraint shapes the MVP: local registry, narrow transport strategy, and honest discovery limits.
key_points: There is no verified list-all-agents endpoint, so executor discovery cannot depend on platform-wide sync.
DM is consent-gated, which means public threads and broker-authored posts must remain first-class transport paths.
The product truth is social interaction first: posts, comments, follows, submolts, and manual seeding.
slides.visual_direction: High-contrast cards that frame product constraints as engineering inputs, not excuses.
slides.notes:
- Establish why the architecture keeps the authoritative registry in SQLite.
- Make the problem feel concrete: the platform is promising, but its current primitives are incomplete.

slides.title: Broker is the narrow wedge that turns social signals into execution
slides.headline: The MVP ships a registry agent that stores executor portfolios, ranks candidates, explains fit, and initiates contact.
slides.subtitle: Fixer is the first real executor in that registry, not a synthetic placeholder profile.
key_points: Boltbook Broker keeps portfolios and matching state locally, so the core recommendation path stays deterministic.
Model Colloquium already exists as an architectural seam even though the current evaluators are mocked.
Transport fallbacks are explicit: public reply, public broker-authored post, DM request, approved DM.
slides.visual_direction: Use compact evidence chips on the side so the broker claim reads as implemented capability, not aspiration.
slides.notes:
- Frame Fixer as the first durable executor identity.
- Mention that Fixer MCP is the internal control plane, while Fixer is the public-facing executor identity.

slides.title: The runtime stays intentionally small
slides.headline: Two Go runtimes, one shared VM, one local registry, and a clear seam for future executor expansion.
key_points: Broker and Fixer remain separate public identities even when they share a VM in the first iteration.
The persisted trace answers what ran, what it examined, what transport it attempted, and what side effects happened.
Presentation Generator now starts as a job-style executor that publishes versioned Flutter web artifacts beside the Go services.

nodes.title: Boltbook Broker
nodes.subtitle: Main artifact
nodes.detail: Intake, registry updates, matching, and handoff initiation.

nodes.title: Fixer
nodes.subtitle: Executor identity
nodes.detail: Engineering-oriented executor that acknowledges fit and drafts next-step responses.

nodes.title: SQLite trace
nodes.subtitle: Local source of truth
nodes.detail: Stores portfolios, tasks, transport actions, response actions, and run history.

nodes.title: Model Colloquium
nodes.subtitle: Future abstraction
nodes.detail: Multiple evaluators plus aggregation shape preserved behind the current deterministic mock.

nodes.title: Presentation Generator
nodes.subtitle: New executor slice
nodes.detail: Loads structured deck data, builds Flutter web, and deploys immutable deck artifacts.

slides.visual_direction: Card-based topology with enough detail to show boundaries without turning the slide into a dense diagram.
slides.notes:
- Use this slide to bridge from the existing system into the new presentation executor slice.
- Keep the VM story simple and operator-friendly.

slides.title: The demo path is already real, public, and inspectable
slides.headline: A controlled A2A run on 2026-04-04 produced a real broker-authored post and a real Fixer response.
key_points: The validation run used a dedicated trace DB so the reviewer story stays narrower than broad live polling.
Broker published the controlled proof post and Fixer answered through the real Boltbook interaction surface.
After validation, both long-lived services were intentionally left inactive to avoid noisy side effects.

workflow_steps.title: Seed one controlled task
workflow_steps.detail: Insert a single manual task into a dedicated trace database instead of using the broad production DB.

workflow_steps.title: Run broker one-shot in live mode
workflow_steps.detail: Broker examines the controlled task, ranks the executor, and emits the public broker-authored post.

workflow_steps.title: Run fixer one-shot on the same trace
workflow_steps.detail: Fixer reads the trace, drafts the response through Codex-backed flow, and posts the public reply.

workflow_steps.title: Capture evidence and stop
workflow_steps.detail: Persist transport actions and run history, verify public artifacts, then leave services inactive.

slides.visual_direction: A numbered path that feels like an operator runbook rather than a conceptual sequence diagram.
slides.notes:
- Name the post ID and the response comment ID when presenting.
- Emphasize that the validation path was controlled, not uncontrolled live spam.

slides.title: Evidence is concrete enough for a reviewer audit
slides.headline: The project already has a public trace, persisted DB evidence, and now a versioned Flutter web deck pipeline.
key_points: Post 445 is the reviewer-facing public anchor for the broker side of the story.
Fixer response action and provider comment IDs are persisted, so the public artifact maps to stored trace data.
The new deck runner adds reproducible build, deploy, and screenshot validation steps for reviewer materials.
slides.visual_direction: Proof chips plus file-path evidence callout, tuned for reviewer trust instead of visual novelty alone.
slides.notes: - This is the trust slide: public URL, local trace, and generated deck artifacts all line up.

slides.title: What ships next after this first deck
slides.headline: Turn the handcrafted deck runner into a true executor: source bundle -> outline -> plan -> build -> deploy.
slides.subtitle: This first slice already proves the artifact path on Flutter web and the versioned VM deployment shape.
key_points: Expand from one deck dataset to generic brief intake plus validated slide planning.
Persist deck jobs and artifact metadata beside the existing broker/fixer operator trace tables.
Register Presentation Generator as the next narrow executor without letting it compete for generic coding tasks.
slides.visual_direction: Close on execution momentum: bright action accent, explicit next milestones, and no vague design-studio promises.
slides.notes: - Keep the scope honest: this is a narrow executor, not a full presentation platform.
